Name: Oddish
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Flavor-Text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Bellossom
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

Name: Scyther
Flavor-Text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

Name: Pinsir
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Chikorita
Flavor-Text: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sun rays.

Name: Bayleef
Flavor-Text: A spicy aroma emanates from around its neck. The aroma acts as a stimulant to restore health.

Name: Meganium
Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The aroma that rises from its petals contains a substance that calms aggressive feelings.

Name: Ledyba
Flavor-Text: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

Name: Ledian
Flavor-Text: It’s said that the patterns on its back are related to the stars in the night sky, but the details of that relationship remain unclear.

Name: Hoppip
Flavor-Text: Outside of cold regions, Hoppip can be found practically everywhere. This is because the wind carries this species all over the world.

Name: Skiploom
Flavor-Text: Skiploom enthusiasts can apparently tell where a Skiploom was born by the scent drifting from the flower on the Pokémon’s head.

Name: Jumpluff
Ability-Effect: Your Active Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Jumpluff travels on seasonal winds. Once its cotton spores run out, its journey ends, as does its life.

Name: Sunkern
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It suddenly falls out of the sky in the morning. Knowing it’s weak, it simply feeds until it evolves.

Name: Sunflora
Flavor-Text: In the daytime, it rushes about in a hectic manner, but it comes to a complete stop when the sun sets.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

Name: Yanmega
Flavor-Text: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes’ heads instantly while flying by at high speed.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It looks just like a pinecone. Its shell protects it from bird Pokémon that peck it by mistake.

Name: Shuckle ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

Name: Heracross
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.

Name: Cherubi
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, attacks used by this Pokémon cost 1 less [G] Energy.
Effects: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It nimbly dashes about to avoid getting pecked by bird Pokémon that would love to make off with its small, nutrient-rich storage ball.

Name: Cherrim
Flavor-Text: After absorbing plenty of sunlight, Cherrim takes this form. It’s full of energy while it’s like this, and its liveliness will go on until sundown.

Name: Vulpix
Flavor-Text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live 1,000 years, and each of its tails is loaded with supernatural powers.

Name: Cyndaquil
Flavor-Text: It is timid and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

Name: Quilava
Flavor-Text: Before battle, it turns its back on its opponent to demonstrate how ferociously its fire blazes.

Name: Typhlosion
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Burned.
Flavor-Text: If its rage peaks, it becomes so hot that anything that touches it will instantly go up in flames.

Name: Slugma
Flavor-Text: A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: 1 other Pokémon (either yours or your opponent's) is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.
Flavor-Text: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

Name: Magby
Effects: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is still small and timid. Whenever Magby gets excited or surprised, flames leak from its mouth and its nose.

Name: Entei
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 random Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that races across the land. It is said that one is born every time a new volcano appears.

Name: Ho-Oh ex
Effects: Take a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to your Benched Basic Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Darumaka
Flavor-Text: It derives its power from fire burning inside its body. If the fire dwindles, this Pokémon will immediately fall asleep.

Name: Darmanitan
Flavor-Text: The thick arms of this hot-blooded Pokémon can deliver punches capable of obliterating a dump truck.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Burned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There’s a hole in its tail that allows it to draw in the air it needs to keep its fire burning. If the hole gets blocked, this Pokémon will fall ill.

Name: Poliwag
Flavor-Text: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.

Name: Poliwhirl
Flavor-Text: Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

Name: Politoed
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, attacks used by your Pokémon that evolve from Poliwhirl do +40 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At nightfall, these Pokémon appear on the shores of lakes. They announce their territorial claims by letting out cries that sound like shouting.

Name: Horsea
Flavor-Text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

Name: Seadra
Flavor-Text: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

Name: Kingdra ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: You may discard any number of your Benched [W] Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Totodile
Flavor-Text: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

Name: Croconaw
Flavor-Text: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

Name: Marill
Flavor-Text: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.

Name: Azumarill
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks from [R] or [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.

Name: Wooper
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

Name: Quagsire
Flavor-Text: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn’t care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

Name: Qwilfish
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Be cautious if this Pokémon starts sucking in water—it will soon attack by scattering the toxic spikes that grow all over its body.

Name: Corsola
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It will regrow any branches that break off its head. People keep particularly beautiful Corsola branches as charms to promote safe childbirth.

Name: Remoraid
Flavor-Text: The water they shoot from their mouths can hit moving prey from more than 300 feet away.

Name: Octillery
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen. This effect lasts until the Defending Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, and it doesn't stack.
Flavor-Text: Its instinct is to bury itself in holes. It often steals the nesting holes of others to sleep in them.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

Name: Mantine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

Name: Suicune
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.
Flavor-Text: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

Name: Corphish
Flavor-Text: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

Name: Crawdaunt
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.

Name: Ducklett
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

Name: Swanna
Effects: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

Name: Chinchou
Flavor-Text: Its antennae, which evolved from a fin, have both positive and negative charges flowing through them.

Name: Lanturn ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Pichu
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

Name: Flaaffy
Flavor-Text: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won’t grow.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The bright light on its tail can be seen far away. It has been treasured since ancient times as a beacon.

Name: Elekid
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When a storm approaches, this Pokémon gets restless. Once Elekid hears the sound of thunder, it gets full-on rowdy.

Name: Raikou
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The rain clouds it carries let it fire thunderbolts at will. They say that it descended with lightning.

Name: Emolga
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.
Flavor-Text: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Slowking
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.

Name: Smoochum
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its face gets even slightly dirty, Smoochum will bathe immediately. But if its body gets dirty, Smoochum doesn’t really seem to care.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Cleffa
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: According to local rumors, Cleffa are often seen in places where shooting stars have fallen.

Name: Togepi
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.
Flavor-Text: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

Name: Togetic
Flavor-Text: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

Name: Natu
Flavor-Text: It is extremely good at climbing tree trunks and likes to eat the new sprouts on the trees.

Name: Xatu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP is now 10.
Flavor-Text: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

Name: Espeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Unown
Ability-Effect: This Ability works if you have any Unown in play with an Ability other than GUARD. All of your Pokémon take −10 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

Name: Unown
Ability-Effect: This Ability works if you have any Unown in play with an Ability other than POWER. Attacks used by your Pokémon do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn while it was in the Active Spot, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to pump up its counterstrike.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Girafarig’s tail has a small head. It instinctively bites at any foe that approaches the Pokémon from behind.

Name: Snubbull
Flavor-Text: It grows close to others easily and is also easily spoiled. The disparity between its face and its actions makes many young people wild about it.

Name: Granbull
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: While it has powerful jaws, it doesn’t care for disputes, so it rarely has a chance to display their might.

Name: Munna
Flavor-Text: Late at night, it appears beside people’s pillows. As it feeds on dreams, the patterns on its body give off a faint glow.

Name: Musharna
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When dark mists emanate from its body, don’t get too near. If you do, your nightmares will become reality.

Name: Onix
Flavor-Text: As it digs through the ground, it absorbs many hard objects. This is what makes its body so solid.

Name: Sudowoodo
Flavor-Text: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.

Name: Gligar
Flavor-Text: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

Name: Gliscor
Flavor-Text: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Swinub
Flavor-Text: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

Name: Piloswine
Flavor-Text: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

Name: Phanpy
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

Name: Donphan ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Tyrogue
Flavor-Text: It is always bursting with energy. To make itself stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

Name: Larvitar
Flavor-Text: Born deep underground, this Pokémon  becomes a pupa after eating enough  dirt to make a mountain.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

Name: Binacle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

Name: Barbaracle
Flavor-Text: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

Name: Crobat ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Spinarak
Flavor-Text: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

Name: Ariados
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: Every night, it wanders around in search of prey, whose movements it restrains by spewing threads before it bites into them with its fangs.

Name: Umbreon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Honchkrow
Flavor-Text: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

Name: Weavile
Flavor-Text: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.
Flavor-Text: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

Name: Tyranitar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [D] Energy from each of your Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn’t care about others.

Name: Absol
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

Name: Forretress
Flavor-Text: It remains immovably rooted to its tree. It scatters pieces of its hard shell to drive its enemies away.

Name: Steelix
Flavor-Text: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.

Name: Scizor
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object they get ahold of into bits.

Name: Skarmory ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

Name: Mawile
Flavor-Text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

Name: Klink
Flavor-Text: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

Name: Klang
Flavor-Text: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon’s rotation speed increases sharply.

Name: Klinklang
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Gear Spinner attack does +70 damage.
Flavor-Text: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

Name: Spearow
Flavor-Text: Its reckless nature leads it to stand up to others—even large Pokémon—if it has to protect its territory.

Name: Fearow
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a [G] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

Name: Blissey
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who takes even one taste of Blissey’s egg becomes unfailingly caring and pleasant to everyone.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Porygon
Flavor-Text: State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

Name: Porygon2
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to this Pokémon, put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

Name: Sentret
Flavor-Text: When acting as a lookout, it warns others of danger by screeching and hitting the ground with its tail.

Name: Furret
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The mother puts its offspring to sleep by curling up around them. It corners foes with speed.

Name: Hoothoot
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can't be Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.
Flavor-Text: As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.

Name: Ambipom
Flavor-Text: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its paws are soaked in oodles of honey. When nervous, Teddiursa will lick its paws and soon have a smile back on its face.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It usually wears a hardened expression, but when it’s licking up honey—which it loves—the joy it feels will cause it to break into a wide grin.

Name: Stantler
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon apparently used to live in much harsher environments, and thus it once had stronger psychic powers than it does now.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Change the type of a random Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of the following at random: [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], or [M].
Flavor-Text: The fluid of Smeargle’s tail secretions changes in the intensity of its hue as the Pokémon’s emotions change.

Name: Lugia ex
Effects: Discard a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Bouffalant
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

Name: Elemental Switch
Description: Move a [R], [W], or [L] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Squirt Bottle
Description: Discard a [R] Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Steel Apron
Description: The [M] Pokémon this card is attached to takes −10 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon, recovers from all Special Conditions, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

Name: Dark Pendant
Description: If the [D] Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.

Name: Rescue Scarf
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, put it into your hand instead of the discard pile.

Name: Will
Description: The next time you flip any number of coins for the effect of an attack, Ability, or Trainer card after using this card on this turn, the first coin flip will definitely be heads.

Name: Lyra
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon that has damage on it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Silver
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and shuffle it into your opponent's deck.

Name: Fisher
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, a [W] Pokémon is chosen at random from your discard pile and put into your hand.

Name: Jasmine
Description: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Steelix and Skarmory ex take −50 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Hiker
Description: For each of your [F] Pokémon in play, look at that many cards from the top of your deck and put them back in any order.

Name: Chikorita
Flavor-Text: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up sun rays.

Name: Bellossom
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

Name: Heracross
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.

Name: Cyndaquil
Flavor-Text: It is timid and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

Name: Magby
Effects: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is still small and timid. Whenever Magby gets excited or surprised, flames leak from its mouth and its nose.

Name: Totodile
Flavor-Text: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

Name: Qwilfish
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Be cautious if this Pokémon starts sucking in water—it will soon attack by scattering the toxic spikes that grow all over its body.

Name: Octillery
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen. This effect lasts until the Defending Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, and it doesn't stack.
Flavor-Text: Its instinct is to bury itself in holes. It often steals the nesting holes of others to sleep in them.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

Name: Pichu
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The bright light on its tail can be seen far away. It has been treasured since ancient times as a beacon.

Name: Togepi
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.
Flavor-Text: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

Name: Xatu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP is now 10.
Flavor-Text: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn while it was in the Active Spot, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to pump up its counterstrike.

Name: Gligar
Flavor-Text: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

Name: Spinarak
Flavor-Text: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Tyranitar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [D] Energy from each of your Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn’t care about others.

Name: Scizor
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object they get ahold of into bits.

Name: Sentret
Flavor-Text: When acting as a lookout, it warns others of danger by screeching and hitting the ground with its tail.

Name: Hoothoot
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can't be Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Stantler
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon apparently used to live in much harsher environments, and thus it once had stronger psychic powers than it does now.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Change the type of a random Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of the following at random: [G], [R], [W], [L], [P], [F], [D], or [M].
Flavor-Text: The fluid of Smeargle’s tail secretions changes in the intensity of its hue as the Pokémon’s emotions change.

Name: Blissey
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Anyone who takes even one taste of Blissey’s egg becomes unfailingly caring and pleasant to everyone.

Name: Shuckle ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

Name: Ho-Oh ex
Effects: Take a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to your Benched Basic Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Kingdra ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Lanturn ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Espeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Donphan ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Crobat ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Umbreon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

Name: Skarmory ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

Name: Lugia ex
Effects: Discard a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Will
Description: The next time you flip any number of coins for the effect of an attack, Ability, or Trainer card after using this card on this turn, the first coin flip will definitely be heads.

Name: Lyra
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon that has damage on it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Silver
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a Supporter card you find there and shuffle it into your opponent's deck.

Name: Fisher
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, a [W] Pokémon is chosen at random from your discard pile and put into your hand.

Name: Jasmine
Description: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Steelix and Skarmory ex take −50 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Hiker
Description: For each of your [F] Pokémon in play, look at that many cards from the top of your deck and put them back in any order.

Name: Shuckle ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

Name: Kingdra ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Lanturn ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Espeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Donphan ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Crobat ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Umbreon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

Name: Skarmory ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

Name: Ho-Oh ex
Effects: Take a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to your Benched Basic Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Lugia ex
Effects: Discard a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

Name: Flareon
Effects: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: You may discard any number of your Benched [W] Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Misdreavus
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

Name: Primeape
Effects: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

Name: Nidoran♀
Effects: Put 1 random Nidoran♂ from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Females are more sensitive to smells than males. While foraging, they’ll use their whiskers to check wind direction and stay downwind of predators.

Name: Nidorina
Flavor-Text: The horn on its head has atrophied. It’s thought that this happens so Nidorina’s children won’t get poked while their mother is feeding them.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your Benched Nidoking.
Flavor-Text: Nidoqueen is better at defense than offense. With scales like armor, this Pokémon will shield its children from any kind of attack.

Name: Nidoran♂
Flavor-Text: The horn on a male Nidoran’s forehead contains a powerful poison. This is a very cautious Pokémon, always straining its large ears.

Name: Nidorino
Flavor-Text: With a horn that’s harder than diamond, this Pokémon goes around shattering boulders as it searches for a moon stone.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: When it goes on a rampage, it’s impossible to control. But in the presence of a Nidoqueen it’s lived with for a long time, Nidoking calms down.

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

Name: Lickitung
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Yanmega ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Leafeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may take a [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your [G] Pokémon.

Name: Gyarados ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Glaceon ex
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Pachirisu ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

Name: Mismagius ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Weavile ex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

Name: Lickilicky ex
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

Name: Ho-Oh ex
Effects: Take a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to your Benched Basic Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Lugia ex
Effects: Discard a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from this Pokémon.
